item.title: SpaceX aims to file for IPO as soon as this week, The Information reports
item.description: SpaceX is aiming ​to file its initial ‌public offering prospectus with regulators later this week ​or next week, ​The Information reported on Tuesday, ⁠citing a person ​with direct knowledge of the ​plans.

item.title: CK Hutchison unit says Panama arbitration claim now tops $2 billion
item.description: A unit of Hong Kong conglomerate CK Hutchison said on Tuesday it had widened its claims in an ​international arbitration case against Panama, saying damages had now risen to ‌more than $2 billion.

item.title: Openreach taps Google AI to speed fibre rollout, cut emissions
item.description: Openreach has expanded its partnership with Google Cloud to use artificial ​intelligence to speed up fibre broadband construction ‌and cut emissions from one of Britain's largest commercial vehicle fleets, the BT-owned network operator said on Wednesday.

item.title: Anduril, Palantir developing Golden Dome missile shield's software, source says
item.description: Anduril and Palantir Technologies are working together to develop software for ​U.S. President Donald Trump's Golden Dome antimissile shield initiative, ‌a source familiar with the matter said on Tuesday.

item.title: US oil prices fall on prospect of Middle East ceasefire easing supply disruption
item.description: U.S. crude futures fell about 4% in early trade on Wednesday on ​the prospect of a possible ceasefire easing ‌disruption to global oil supply, following reports that Washington had sent Tehran a 15-point plan to ​end the war in the Middle East.

item.title: SK Hynix files confidentiality for 2026 US listing
item.description: South Korea's SK Hynix said on ​Wednesday it has submitted a ‌confidential filing to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for a possible listing ​of American depository receipts in ​2026.

item.title: US shale firms unlikely to drill at $100 a barrel unless high prices last longer, executives say
item.description: Oil prices above $100 a barrel would not trigger a meaningful production increase ​in the U.S. unless they stay elevated for more than a quarter, shale executives said at the CERAWeek energy conference in ‌Houston, troubling news for consumers slammed by the energy crisis during the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Britain pilots social media bans, time limits and curfews for children
item.description: Hundreds of British families will test social media bans, curfews and app time ​limits to see how they impact ‌children's sleep, family life and schoolwork, the government said.

item.title: Fed's Barr sees rates holding steady 'for some time' given above-target inflation
item.description: The U.S. Federal Reserve may need to keep interest rates steady "for some time" before ​further cuts are warranted, Fed Governor Michael Barr said ‌on Tuesday, noting continued inflation above the Fed's 2% target and the risks posed by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Baltimore sues Elon Musk's xAI over Grok sexual 'deepfakes'
item.description: The city of Baltimore sued Elon Musk’s xAI on Tuesday, claiming ​its Grok chatbot illegally generates nonconsensual sexually explicit images, including of children.

item.title: Grifols approves IPO of its US biopharma business
item.description: Spanish drugmaker Grifols said on ​Tuesday it has approved a U.S. initial ‌public offering of its U.S. biopharma business.

item.title: Jury orders Meta to pay $375 mln in New Mexico lawsuit over child sexual exploitation, user safety
item.description: A New Mexico jury on Tuesday found Meta Platforms violated state law in a lawsuit brought by the state attorney general, who accused the company of misleading users about the safety of Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp and of enabling child sexual exploitation on those platforms.

item.title: Trading Day: This ain't over
item.description: Wall Street brought an otherwise positive day for global stocks to a downbeat end on Tuesday, as rebounding oil prices, spiking bond yields and soft business activity data compounded concerns that the war in the Middle East is far from over.

item.title: GameStop posts 14% fall in quarterly revenue amid digital gaming shift
item.description: GameStop reported a 14% drop in fourth-quarter revenue on Tuesday, a sign that its struggling brick-and-mortar business continues to face immense ​pressure from the video game industry's shift to digital downloads ‌and weaker consumer demand.

item.title: MercadoLibre to invest $11 billion in Brazil this year
item.description: E-commerce firm MercadoLibre ​will invest 57 billion reais ($10.9 billion) ‌in its main market Brazil this year, up 50% from 2025, it said on Tuesday.

item.title: Robinhood announces $1.5 billion share buyback
item.description: Trading ​platform ‌Robinhood ​said ​on ⁠Tuesday ​it ​has approved ​a ​new $1.5 billion ‌share ⁠repurchase ​program.

item.title: NASA awards Intuitive Machines $180.4 million lunar payload contract
item.description: Intuitive Machines said on Tuesday NASA has awarded the space ​startup a $180.4 million contract to deliver ‌seven science and technology payloads to the moon's south pole.

item.title: OpenAI set to discontinue Sora video platform
item.description: OpenAI is ‌discontinuing its video generation tool ​Sora, according ​to a post ⁠on social ​media site X ​on Tuesday.

item.title: UPS withdraws latest driver buyout scheme in central region states
item.description: United Parcel Service notified the International Brotherhood of Teamsters that it was ​withdrawing its latest driver buyout scheme ‌in central region states, the union said on Tuesday.

item.title: US lawmakers ask whether Nvidia CEO's smuggling remarks misled regulators
item.description: Two U.S. senators have asked U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick to investigate whether remarks by Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang may have misled U.S. officials and ​influenced their decision to grant Nvidia licenses to send its AI chips to ‌China.

item.title: Co-founder of defense tech firm Anduril says US legislative failures are giving China a strategic edge
item.description: Trae Stephens, co-founder of defense technology firm Anduril Industries, sharply criticized ​American lawmakers on Tuesday, warning that congressional dysfunction and Silicon Valley arrogance were handing China a strategic opening in the ‌race for military and technological supremacy.

item.title: CERAWEEK Rio Tinto expects Resolution Copper mine to open by mid-2030s
item.description: Rio Tinto expects to open Arizona's Resolution Copper mine ​by the mid-2030s, a senior executive of ‌the miner told Reuters on Tuesday.

item.title: Arm unveils new AI chip, expects it to add billions in annual revenue
item.description: Arm Holdings announced a new artificial intelligence data center chip on Tuesday which ​it said will add billions of dollars of revenue and represent a significant shift ‌in the company's strategy.

item.title: Brazil audit court sidesteps Banco Master ruling as parallel investigations unfold
item.description: Brazil's federal audit court TCU deferred its ruling on the Banco Master case despite the conclusion of an internal audit amid elements under review by ​other authorities that are necessary for its decision, the judge overseeing the ‌case said on Tuesday.

item.title: Estee Lauder's bet on Puig is bold fragrance play in a volatile world
item.description: Estee Lauder's potential combination with Puig Brands would put it in a direct fight with sector leader L'Oreal's premium fragrances, but could complicate the ​U.S. company's turnaround plans just as the Iran war clouds the outlook for travel retail.

item.title: CVS reaches insulin pricing settlement with FTC
item.description: CVS Health said on Tuesday it has reached a proposed settlement agreement with the Federal Trade ​Commission on insulin pricing.

item.title: EU antitrust chief meets Google, Meta, OpenAI, Amazon CEOs amidst AI scrutiny
item.description: EU antitrust chief Teresa Ribera, who has voiced concerns ​about Big Tech extending its dominance into ‌artificial intelligence, will meet the CEOs of Alphabet , Meta Platforms and OpenAI on Tuesday, a European ​Commission agenda item shows.

item.title: Delta suspends special treatment for Congress as shutdown sows chaos in airports
item.description: Delta Air Lines cited the impact ​of a partial government shutdown that has disrupted U.S. air travel.

item.title: Microsoft to rent Texas data center dropped by Oracle and OpenAI, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Microsoft has agreed to rent a data center project in Texas that ​was originally being developed for Oracle ‌and OpenAI, Bloomberg News reported on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the situation.

item.title: OpenAI non-profit names leaders, plans to spend $1 billion this year, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: OpenAI is ​making several ‌key hires to ​lead ​its non-profit arm ⁠and ​is ​planning to spend $1 billion ​in ​various AI-related investments ‌this ⁠year through the non-profit ​unit, ​Bloomberg ⁠News reported ​on ​Tuesday.
